heading: Appropriations for travel: may not be used for attendance at certain meetings
label: 455 Appropriations for travel: may not be used for attendance at certain meetings
text: Appropriations of the Department of Defense that are available for travel may not, without the approval of the Secretary concerned or his designee, be used for expenses incident to attendance of a member of an armed force under that department at a meeting of a technical, scientific, professional, or similar organization.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4125:174a., .Aug. 1, 1953, ch. 305, § 60567 Stat. 349The words “may not . . . be used” are substituted for the words “shall not be available”. The words “on and after ” are omitted as executed. The words “Secretary concerned” are substituted for the words “Secretary of the department concerned” to conform to other sections of this revised title and to the definition in section 101(5) of this revised title. So much of the source statute as relates to civilian employees is omitted as superseded by the Act of , , .August 1, 1953July 7, 1958Pub. L. 85–50772 Stat. 327

Prior ProvisionsAct , cited as the source of this section in the Historical and Revision Notes above, is known as the Department of Defense Appropriation Act, 1954.
